title: Weekly Market Update: Stocks Gain 0.9% as Energy Rises and Basic Materials Fall
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The Morningstar US Market Index rose 0.9%, with energy up 3.42% and basic materials down 1.98%. Large-cap stocks gained 1.01%, mid-cap stocks rose 0.75%, and sm
excerpt: The Morningstar US Market Index rose 0.9%, with energy up 3.42% and basic materials down 1.98%. Large-cap stocks gained 1.01%, mid-cap stocks rose 0.75%, and small-cap stocks increased by 0.03%. For more details, visit Morningstar's official website.

content.introduction: The Morningstar US Market Index rose 0.9%, with energy up 3.42% and basic materials down 1.98%. Large-cap stocks gained 1.01%, mid-cap stocks rose 0.75%, and small-cap stocks increased by 0.03%. For more details, visit Morningstar's official website.
body: # Weekly Market Update: Stocks Fall as Energy Rises and Basic Materials Falter. The energy sector ticked up this week, while Celsius and Norwegian Cruise Line lost 19%. ## Stock Market Update for the Trading Week Ended March 6. ## Highlights of This Week’s Market and Investing Events. The author or authors own shares in one or more securities mentioned in this article. ## More in Stocks. Transparency is how we protect the integrity of our work and keep empowering investors to achieve their goals and dreams. And we have unwavering standards for how we keep that integrity intact, from our research and data to our policies on. To learn more about how we handle and protect your data, visit our privacy center. To further protect the integrity of our editorial content, we keep a strict separation between our sales teams and authors to remove any pressure or influence on our analyses and research.

[Bloom Energy Corp Class A (BE)](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnys/be/quote). [Oracle Corp (ORCL)](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnys/orcl/quote). [EchoStar Corp Class A (SATS)](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnas/sats/quote). Technology led the declines, falling 2.18% for the week as AI heavyweights Oracle [ORCL](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnys/orcl/quote) and Broadcom [AVGO](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnas/avgo/quote) fell sharply after earnings.
